title: [ExportVerilog] Expressions substituted into verbatim expressions should not wrap
body:
Current wrapping behavior wraps expressions if they're longer than the configured `emittedLineLength`.

This can result in verbatim statements containing newlines and indentation which does not seem in spirit of verbatim.

Test case:

```mlir
hw.module @VerbatimWrapping(%clock : i1, %cond : i1, %val : i8, %a : i3, %b : i3) {
      %x = comb.add %a, %b : i3
      %y = comb.xor %a, %b : i3
      %arr = hw.array_create %x, %y, %x, %y, %x, %y, %x, %y, %x, %y, %x, %y : i3
      sv.verbatim "// {{0}}" (%arr) : !hw.array<12xi3>
}
```

This verbatim attempts to prefix an expression with `//` to make it a comment.

Currently this is the output of `circt-opt -export-verilog -o /dev/null`:
```systemverilog
// Generated by CIRCT 1.20.0g20221025_97824a4
module VerbatimWrapping(	// ./verbatim-wrapping.mlir:1:1
  input       clock,
              cond,
  input [7:0] val,
  input [2:0] a,
              b);

  wire [2:0] _GEN = a + b;	// ./verbatim-wrapping.mlir:2:12
  wire [2:0] _GEN_0 = a ^ b;	// ./verbatim-wrapping.mlir:3:12
  // {{_GEN}, {_GEN_0}, {_GEN}, {_GEN_0}, {_GEN}, {_GEN_0}, {_GEN}, {_GEN_0}, {_GEN}, {_GEN_0},
                {_GEN}, {_GEN_0}}	// ./verbatim-wrapping.mlir:2:12, :3:12, :4:14, :5:7
endmodule
```

Which is not valid verilog due to the unexpected wrapping occurring.
